before:
---
name: feedback_backlog_nnn_immutable
description: Backlog NNN ids are immutable — never rename/renumber an existing backlog item
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: feedback
  originSessionId: 43f39c1a-cc6a-4f2c-860d-d64a6835fcf8
---

Never rename or renumber an existing `backlog/<NNN>-<slug>.md` file's `NNN`. The number is the item's id for life; only the slug may be reworded (via `formerSlugs:` redirects).

**Why:** Renumbering silently breaks every `#NNN` short-ref and `/backlog/<NNN>/` URL, and under concurrent agents (multiple sessions work this backlog at once) it cascades into id collisions — sessions chasing the same next-number renumber each other's work. Hit live on 2026-06-06: a concurrent agent renumbered an item 117→119 mid-task, colliding with files being restored.

**How to apply:** New item = highest `NNN` + 1 or a never-used gap; re-check `ls backlog/` right before writing and, on a collision, the *newer* item yields to the next free number — never touch a number already on disk. Codified in [docs/agent/backlog-workflow.md](docs/agent/backlog-workflow.md) (*Adding an item*, *Renaming an item*, *Rules*) and the [[feedback_backlog_is_tracker]] skill. Relates to [[feedback_no_commit_talk_in_backlog]].
after:
---
name: feedback_backlog_nnn_immutable
description: Backlog NNN ids are immutable — never rename/renumber an existing backlog item
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: feedback
  originSessionId: 43f39c1a-cc6a-4f2c-860d-d64a6835fcf8
---

Never rename or renumber an existing `backlog/<NNN>-<slug>.md` file's `NNN`. The number is the item's id for life; only the slug may be reworded (via `formerSlugs:` redirects).

**Why:** Renumbering silently breaks every `#NNN` short-ref and `/backlog/<NNN>/` URL, and under concurrent agents (multiple sessions work this backlog at once) it cascades into id collisions — sessions chasing the same next-number renumber each other's work. Hit live on 2026-06-06: a concurrent agent renumbered an item 117→119 mid-task, colliding with files being restored.

**How to apply:** New item = highest `NNN` + 1 or a never-used gap; re-check `ls backlog/` right before writing and, on a collision, the *newer* item yields to the next free number — never touch a number already on disk. Codified in [docs/agent/backlog-workflow.md](docs/agent/backlog-workflow.md) (*Adding an item*, *Renaming an item*, *Rules*) and the [[feedback_backlog_is_tracker]] skill. Relates to [[feedback_no_commit_talk_in_backlog]].

**Resolving a real collision (the one sanctioned renumber):** when two parallel lanes DID land the same `NNN` (an allocation race the disjointness checker can't see), heal it ONLY via `node scripts/backlog-renumber-collisions.mjs` (pure core `scripts/backlog/renumber-collisions.mjs`, #2071) — never `git mv` by hand (guard-bash blocks a shell renumber) and never rename without a full reference sweep. The tool is **reference-aware**: deterministic "newer-yields", allocates a free id, and rewrites EVERY inbound ref (`#NNN` short-refs, `/backlog/NNN[/-]` URLs, `parent:`/`blockedBy:` edges) so nothing dangles. A duplicate id reddens `main` via `check:standards` "ids must be unique" AND `src/_data/__tests__/backlogGraph.test.ts` (a dup makes `loadBacklog`/`backlogGraph` disagree — `expected 'A' to be null`), which jams the whole drain queue (observed 2026-07-04: #2280 landed twice).

**The drain-heal gap:** the merge-time heal runs in `pr-land` (pre-check + post-land) but NOT yet in the drain (`scripts/merge-ai-prs.mjs`) — so a *new* item landed via `/drain` can still collide. #2222/#2276 (PR #151) wires the heal into the lander to close it; until it lands, if the drain reddens `main` on a dup, re-run the renumber CLI + land a hotfix. Relates to [[edit-work-runs-in-a-lane-clone]].
